briefdescription.para: Returns the parent of node. node MUST NOT be NULL.
Sets the parent of node to parent.

detaileddescription.para: If node is NULL, this function has no effect.

briefdescription.para: Returns true when the node's color is red, false otherwise (black or NULL).
Assigns color from other to the node.

detaileddescription.para: If other is NULL assumes color black. If node is NULL, this function has no effect.

briefdescription.para: Marks the node as red. If node is NULL, this function has no effect.
Marks the node as black. If node is NULL, this function has no effect.
Returns the leftmost descendant of node.
Returns the rightmost descendant of node.
Rotates node counter-clockwise (left), i.e., it is replaced in the tree by its right child, while becoming that child's left child.
Rotates node clockwise (right), i.e., it is replaced in the tree by its left child, while becoming that child's right child.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rbtree *tree, const struct rbnode *old_node, struct rbnode *new_node)
memberdef.name: rbtree_replace

briefdescription.para: Replaces old_node by new_node in tree by updating its parent.
Returns a pointer to the previous (in-order) node in a red-black tree with respect to node.

detaileddescription.para: This is, at worst, an O(log(n)) operation.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the next (in-order) node in a red-black tree with respect to node.

detaileddescription.para: This is, at worst, an O(log(n)) operation. However, visiting all nodes in order is an O(n) operation, and therefore, on average, O(1) for each node.

briefdescription.para: Inserts a node into a red-black tree.

detaileddescription.para: This is an O(log(n)) operation. This function does not check whether a node with the same key already exists, or whether the node is already part of another tree.

briefdescription.para: Removes a node from a red-black tree.
Finds a node in a red-black tree.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the node if found, or NULL if not.

briefdescription.para: Checks if a node is part of a red-black tree.

simplesect.para: true if the node was found in the tree, and false if not.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the first (leftmost) node in a red-black tree.
Returns a pointer to the last (rightmost) node in a red-black tree.
This file is part of the utilities library; it contains the implementation of the red-black tree.

detaileddescription.para:
lely/util/rbtree.h
